content:
From Royal Bans to the Modern Green Lawn Bowls is the anglicised variant of a family of ancient rolling sports. Across the globe, this basic concept has taken on numerous distinct regional signatures: Bocce in Italy, Bolla among ancient Saxons, Bolle in Denmark, Boules or Pétanque in France, and Ula Maika in Polynesia.

The standard English variation developed on finely manicured grass lawns—a natural byproduct of England's damp, temperate climate. Conversely, in the hotter climates of France and Italy where pristine grass is harder to sustain, variations evolved to be played primarily on gravel, stone dust, or hard dirt pitches.
